assertions.reason: The browser surface exposed a launchStudy error message for a forbidden-action conflict, but not the raw validateStudyLaunch(study) { pass, reasons[] } result the assertion specifies.
The production launch confirmation modal auto-uses acknowledgement on Confirm Launch; the browser offered no way to submit a production launch without ack and observe mutation-level rejection.
No real run reached blocked_by_guardrail from the browser surface, so a run detail showing the violated rule could not be exercised.
Cumulative-failure auto-cancel requires real run execution and remaining queued runs, which were not reachable from browser-only hardening validation.
Budget-cap enforcement depends on admin settings changes and live execution; neither was reachable from the browser surface.
guardrailEvents auditing is a backend-table assertion and was not directly observable from the browser surface.
Launching a study whose task spec explicitly referenced payment submission surfaced a visible, human-readable failure reason: Task spec references forbidden action "payment_submission".
Subsequent-study effects require admin settings changes, but the browser surface never exposed an admin-capable settings session.

frictions.description: A staging study aimed at forbidden.example advanced to persona review instead of surfacing a domain-allowlist error on a fresh self-service org.
frictions.resolution: Switched to a forbidden-action text conflict (payment_submission) to exercise a browser-visible pre-launch guardrail failure instead.

blockers.description: Assertions that depend on admin settings changes, guardrail audit tables, or real blocked_by_guardrail run records remain unavailable from browser-only validation.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Imported and published a real pack, created studies from the browser, and exercised launch flow with both forbidden.example and payment_submission test data.

summary: Confirmed that a forbidden-action conflict surfaces in the study UI, while the remaining guardrail assertions stayed blocked by admin-only or live-run prerequisites.
